rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR SELF-IMPROVEMENT AND COMMUNITY VS. LENNAR CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANTS LENNAR CORPORATION, LENNAR HOMES OF CALIFORNIA INC, LENNAR-BVHP LLC, LENNAR ASSOCIATES MANAGEMENT LLC, LENNAR COMMUNITIES, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to the fifth cause of action, plaintiff must plead physical injury. As to the sixth cause of action, plaintiff must plead that it expended money due to defendants' acts of unfair competition, lost money or property, has been claimed money to which it has a cognizable claim, there has been a mininution in value of its assets. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MAURA P. LOREDO VS. ALL AMERICAN FINANCIAL GROUP INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT AMERICAN GUARDIAN FINANCIAL GROUP, INC. MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Both matters are off calendar. This case was singly assigned to Judge Sullivan. Temporary Restraining Order to remain in effect through February 10, 2010 so plaintiff can seeek appropriate relief before assigned Judge. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IRENA ROTBART VS. T-MOBILE USA,INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT T-MOBILE USA, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to first, second, third, fourth, fifth, and seventh causes of action due to preemption. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for unfair business practices. Plaintiff to plead facts that demonstrate standing and lack of preemption. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IRENA ROTBART VS. T-MOBILE USA,INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT T-MOBILE USA,INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted, no opposition. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: INMA PENA VS. BLACKROCK INSTITUTIONAL TRUST COMPANY, NA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT BARCLAYS GLOBAL INVESTORS, NA MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication denied as to disability discrimination cause of action. The allegations of this cause of action are within the scope of the administrative investigation which can reasonably be expected to grow out of the charge of discrimination. The initial administrative complaint references "denied accommodation" and that "BGI would not accommodate plaintiff's return from medical leave by reducing her hours." Motion for summary adjudication denied as to retaliation cause of action, failure to shift burden of proof. Insufficient evidence presented that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support retaliation. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK DOWNING et al VS. LFG INVESTMENTS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 5, CROSS DEFENDANTS FIRST AMERICAN TITLE COMPANY OF SONOMA COUNTY, FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Matter to be heard in Department 301, same day and time, per recusal of Judge Woolard. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA ZEVALLOS VS. MARILYN CHAVEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltf'S Verified Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANTS EXCEL REALTY & MORTGAGE INC, JOSEPH CHAVEZ, SHAWNACY NAUROTH Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's Verified Complaint. Motion to strike is granted with ten days leave to amend to allege facts supporting punitive damages claim and entitlement to attorney fees. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA ZEVALLOS VS. MARILYN CHAVEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANTS EXCEL REALTY & MORTGAGE INC, JOSEPH CHAVEZ, SHAWNACY NAUROTH DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to all causes of action. Plaintiff must plead facts to support the causes of action as against defendants Excel Realty, Joseph Chavez and Nauroth. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEEANNA M. BROSSOIT VS. DREXEL BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Drexel Bradshaw And Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. Demurrer To Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 11, Defendants Drexel Bradshaw and Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. Demurrer To Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch). Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to first cause of action plaintiff must allege causation and plead around the unclean hands defense. As to the second cause of action plaintiff must particularly allege defendant's knowledge of falsity and damages. As to the third cause of action, plaintiff must specify why defendant had no reasonable grounds to believe his representations were true and damages. As to the fourth cause of action plaintiff must allege facts regarding the breach of contract terms and damages, and plead around the defense of plaintiff's non-performance. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEEANNA M. BROSSOIT VS. DREXEL BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Drexel Bradshaw And Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. Motion To Strike Complaint And Portions Of Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 11, Defendants Drexel Bradshaw and Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. Motion to Strike Complaint and Portions of Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Challenge To Judge Busch). Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must comply with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.110, 2.111, and 2.112(4). Plaintiff must plead the basis for an award of attorney fees and delete arguments and citations to case law. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTHA SOTO VS. LAMPERTI CONTRACTING & DESIGN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 1, 2010, line 10, CROSS DEFENDANTS MARTHA SOTO, SANTOS SOTO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Court treats demurrer as a motion to strike (CCP section 436.) Plaintiffs improperly named as cross-defendants. Lamperti states no affirmative claims are asserted against the Sotos in the cross-complaint. =(302/CWW)
